title: PRM393 – 2 đề FE + ôn 12 slide (224 câu)
subject: PRM393

questions.question: Which widget pattern protects authenticated routes?
questions.explanation: Mẫu Guard (route guard) chặn/cho phép vào route đã xác thực. Navigator chỉ điều hướng; Middleware là khái niệm web/server, không phải widget pattern Flutter chuẩn.

questions.question: Why are tests important for refactoring?
questions.explanation: Test đảm bảo khi refactor, hành vi app không bị hỏng. Không liên quan giảm UI work hay animation.

questions.question: What is the role of BuildContext in Flutter?

options.B: Locate widgets in the widget tree

questions.explanation: BuildContext dùng để định vị widget trong cây, tìm ancestor (Theme, Navigator, MediaQuery…). Không lưu data app hay vẽ pixel.

questions.question: What role does the Flutter framework play in application development?

options.B: It provides UI components and rendering logic

questions.explanation: Flutter cung cấp UI components + engine render. Không thay OS, không chạy backend/DB server.

questions.question: How is the Flutter UI constructed internally?
questions.explanation: UI Flutter được mô tả bằng cây widget phân cấp (widget tree). Không dùng XML/HTML/CSS layout như Android/web cổ điển.

questions.question: Which widget enables custom scroll effects in Flutter?
questions.explanation: CustomScrollView cho phép sliver + hiệu ứng scroll tùy biến. ListView/SingleChildScrollView đơn giản hơn; Column không scroll.

questions.question: Which practice improves rendering performance?
questions.explanation: Tách nhỏ widget giảm rebuild không cần thiết, cải thiện performance. Cây lớn, global state, rebuild liên tục làm chậm.

questions.question: What does an integration test verify?
questions.explanation: Integration test kiểm tra tương tác nhiều phần / hành vi app end-to-end. Unit test = hàm đơn; widget test = render widget.

questions.question: Why is separating UI and state logic recommended?
questions.explanation: Tách UI và state logic giúp bảo trì, test dễ, giảm coupling. Không chủ yếu vì animation hay tốc độ thuần.

questions.question: When does Flutter rebuild the widget tree?
questions.explanation: Flutter rebuild khi state thay đổi (setState, notifyListeners, emit state…). Không rebuild mỗi frame trừ khi animation/ticker yêu cầu.

questions.question: Which concept does BLoC heavily rely on?
questions.explanation: BLoC dựa mạnh trên Stream (event in → state out). Future dùng một lần; không phải Theme/Animation.

questions.question: Why is version control critical?
questions.explanation: Version control theo dõi và quản lý thay đổi mã nguồn, cộng tác, rollback.

questions.question: Why is immutability important in BLoC states?
questions.explanation: State bất biến giúp thay đổi state có thể dự đoán, so sánh dễ, tránh side-effect khi mutate.

questions.question: Which Flutter plugin provides SQLite support?
questions.explanation: sqflite là plugin SQLite phổ biến trên Flutter. Hive = NoSQL local; shared_preferences = key-value đơn giản.

questions.question: What is the main benefit of using Maps in Dart?
questions.explanation: Map trong Dart lưu cặp key-value.

questions.question: Which tool helps analyze UI performance in Flutter?
questions.explanation: Flutter DevTools phân tích performance UI, timeline, memory. Inspector chủ yếu cây widget.

questions.question: What does a Dart class primarily represent?
questions.explanation: Class là bản thiết kế (blueprint) để tạo object — OOP cơ bản.

questions.question: What is the purpose of SliverAppBar?
questions.explanation: SliverAppBar là app bar linh hoạt, co giãn theo scroll trong CustomScrollView.

questions.question: What is Hive mainly used for?
questions.explanation: Hive: lưu trữ NoSQL local nhẹ, nhanh, không cần native SQL bridge nặng.

questions.question: Which HTTP header is commonly used for authorization?
questions.explanation: Header Authorization mang token/credentials. Content-Type mô tả body; Accept chọn response type.

questions.question: What does TextEditingController manage?
questions.explanation: TextEditingController quản lý giá trị và selection của ô text input.

questions.question: Why is Dart considered suitable for Flutter development?

options.A: It is only used for backend systems
options.B: It supports both UI and application logic efficiently
options.D: It does not support asynchronous programming

questions.explanation: Dart hỗ trợ cả logic app và UI (null-safety, async, hot reload) nên phù hợp Flutter.

questions.question: What does the build() method return in a widget?

options.B: A widget tree describing the UI

questions.explanation: build() trả về widget tree mô tả UI cần render, không trả pixel hay state.

questions.question: What represents the output of a BLoC?
questions.explanation: BLoC nhận Events, phát States. Output chính là States.

questions.question: What is Cubit in BLoC?
questions.explanation: Cubit là BLoC đơn giản hơn: emit state trực tiếp, không cần Event class.

questions.question: Which activity is typically performed after creating a new Flutter project?

options.B: Running the default app on a device

questions.explanation: Sau khi tạo project, bước điển hình là chạy app mặc định trên device/emulator để kiểm tra môi trường.

questions.question: Which practice improves deployment security?
questions.explanation: Bảo mật deploy: bảo vệ signing keys, không hardcode secret, dùng release build đã ký.

questions.question: Why is Chapter 14 considered optional (Plus)?

options.B: Apps can be tested without publishing

questions.explanation: Chương deploy thường optional vì có thể test app đầy đủ mà chưa publish store.

questions.question: What is the primary purpose of ThemeData in Flutter?
questions.explanation: ThemeData định nghĩa style toàn app (màu, font, component theme).

questions.question: Which method is used to update the UI when state changes?
questions.explanation: setState() đánh dấu StatefulWidget cần rebuild UI khi state đổi. build() được gọi lại; initState/dispose là lifecycle.

questions.question: Which Flutter package is commonly used for simple key-value storage?
questions.explanation: shared_preferences lưu key-value đơn giản (bool/int/string). sqflite cho quan hệ; provider là state management.

questions.question: Why should architecture be decided early?
questions.explanation: Chọn architecture sớm giảm chi phí refactor sau này khi app lớn.

questions.question: What is pagination used for?
questions.explanation: Pagination tải dữ liệu theo trang, xử lý dataset lớn hiệu quả, giảm memory/network.

questions.question: Which widget helps avoid rebuilding unchanged UI parts?
questions.explanation: RepaintBoundary cô lập vùng repaint, tránh vẽ lại phần UI không đổi.

questions.question: Which widget is used to handle asynchronous API responses in UI?
questions.explanation: FutureBuilder lắng nghe Future (API) và rebuild UI theo ConnectionState/data/error.

questions.question: Which Dart feature helps organize code into reusable libraries?
questions.explanation: package + import tổ chức code thành thư viện tái sử dụng.

questions.question: What is refresh token used for?
questions.explanation: Refresh token dùng để lấy access token mới, kéo dài phiên mà không login lại.

questions.question: What is rebuild scope optimization in Flutter?
questions.explanation: Rebuild scope optimization: chỉ rebuild widget bị ảnh hưởng, không rebuild cả cây.

questions.question: Why is BLoC suitable for large teams?
questions.explanation: BLoC chuẩn hóa luồng event/state, dễ phân chia việc trong team lớn.

questions.question: Which widget reacts to async data from local storage?
questions.explanation: FutureBuilder phù hợp data async one-shot từ local storage (Future). StreamBuilder cho stream liên tục.

questions.question: What is a token in authentication systems?
questions.explanation: Token là credential tạm thời chứng minh đã xác thực (JWT, bearer…).

questions.question: What does a Tween define?
questions.explanation: Tween định nghĩa khoảng giá trị bắt đầu–kết thúc cho animation.

questions.question: Which Dart feature allows defining reusable, type-safe components?
questions.explanation: Generics cho component/type-safe tái sử dụng (List<T>, class Box<T>).

questions.question: Which widget animates between two widgets?
questions.explanation: AnimatedSwitcher chuyển động giữa hai child widget khác nhau. Các Animated* khác animate một property.

questions.question: Why should setState() be called sparingly?
questions.explanation: setState() gọi nhiều/không cần thiết gây rebuild thừa → giảm performance.

questions.question: What is test isolation?
questions.explanation: Test isolation: mỗi test case độc lập, không phụ thuộc thứ tự/state test khác.

questions.question: What does crossAxisAlignment control?
questions.explanation: Trong Column (trục chính dọc), crossAxisAlignment điều khiển căn ngang. (Trong Row thì ngược lại.)

questions.question: Which tool is commonly used for version control?
questions.explanation: Git là công cụ version control phổ biến nhất hiện nay.

questions.question: Why is clean architecture beneficial?
questions.explanation: Clean architecture tách layer → dễ bảo trì và kiểm thử. Không chủ yếu vì UI/build nhanh.

questions.question: What is Dart primarily designed for?
questions.explanation: Dart là ngôn ngữ đa mục đích, được tối ưu cho UI client (Flutter).

questions.question: Why is secure authentication critical in mobile apps?
questions.explanation: Xác thực bảo mật nhằm bảo vệ dữ liệu người dùng.

questions.question: Which HTTP method is typically used to retrieve data?
questions.explanation: GET dùng để lấy/retrieve dữ liệu.

questions.question: What happens if a non-nullable variable is not initialized in Dart?
questions.explanation: Biến non-nullable không khởi tạo → lỗi compile-time (null safety).

questions.question: Which command builds a release APK?
questions.explanation: flutter build apk tạo file APK release.

questions.question: Which widget allows overlapping of its child widgets?
questions.explanation: Stack xếp chồng / cho phép overlap child.

questions.question: Which platforms can Flutter applications target using a single codebase?
questions.explanation: Flutter multi-platform: mobile + web + desktop từ một codebase.

questions.question: What is the main risk of improper state management?
questions.explanation: State sai → UI không nhất quán / bug hiển thị.

questions.question: What does Curves/Animation provide?
questions.explanation: Curves định nghĩa đường cong/hành vi animation (easing).

questions.question: How can data be passed to a new screen?
questions.explanation: Cách chuẩn: truyền qua constructor của màn hình mới (hoặc route arguments).

questions.question: What is the purpose of parsing JSON?
questions.explanation: Parse JSON → object/map Dart để dùng trong app.

questions.question: Which phase involves gathering requirements?
questions.explanation: Thu thập yêu cầu thuộc giai đoạn Analysis.

questions.question: Which Flutter tool helps debug network requests?
questions.explanation: Flutter DevTools hỗ trợ debug network, performance, v.v.

questions.question: Which Dart feature supports object-oriented programming?
questions.explanation: OOP trong Dart dựa trên class và object.

questions.question: Which approach helps reduce unnecessary widget rebuilds?
questions.explanation: Cập nhật state hẹp (fine-grained) giảm rebuild thừa.

questions.question: Which action best demonstrates Flutter's reactive UI model?

options.B: Rebuilding UI in response to state changes

questions.explanation: UI reactive: state đổi → rebuild UI.

questions.question: Why are const widgets recommended?
questions.explanation: const widget tái sử dụng instance, giảm chi phí rebuild.

questions.question: Why should animations be disposed properly?
questions.explanation: AnimationController/ticker cần dispose để tránh rò memory.

questions.question: Which Flutter test focuses on UI components?
questions.explanation: Widget test kiểm tra UI components Flutter.

questions.question: Why should database operations be asynchronous?
questions.explanation: DB async để không chặn UI thread.

questions.question: Why is Chapter 13 considered optional (Plus)?

options.B: Apps can work without deep optimization

questions.explanation: Tối ưu sâu (plus) không bắt buộc để app chạy được.

questions.question: Which widget applies a Theme to an entire Flutter application?
questions.explanation: MaterialApp (theme:) áp theme toàn app.

questions.question: What is the purpose of SliverAppBar?
questions.explanation: SliverAppBar: app bar co giãn theo scroll.

questions.question: Which widget is best used to add fixed empty space?
questions.explanation: SizedBox tạo khoảng trống kích thước cố định.

questions.question: Which package supports BLoC architecture?
questions.explanation: Package flutter_bloc hỗ trợ BLoC/Cubit.

questions.question: Which Flutter widget reacts to authentication state changes?
questions.explanation: Auth state dạng stream → StreamBuilder (FutureBuilder cho one-shot).

questions.question: What is technical debt?

options.B: Future cost of poor design decisions

questions.explanation: Technical debt: chi phí tương lai do quyết định thiết kế kém.

questions.question: What is implicit animation?
questions.explanation: Implicit animation (AnimatedOpacity…) không cần AnimationController.

questions.question: Which Dart data structure is best suited for storing an ordered collection of items?
questions.explanation: List: tập hợp có thứ tự.

questions.question: Which Flutter concept supports reactive UI updates?
questions.explanation: Reactive UI dựa trên rebuild widget khi state đổi.

questions.question: Which type of test checks individual functions or classes?
questions.explanation: Unit test kiểm tra hàm/class đơn lẻ.

questions.question: What is the role of events in BLoC?
questions.explanation: Events kích hoạt xử lý → emit state mới.

questions.question: What is the main purpose of hot reload in Flutter?

options.B: Instantly reflect code changes in UI

questions.explanation: Hot reload áp code change lên UI gần như ngay, giữ state.

questions.question: Why are forms important in mobile applications?
questions.explanation: Form dùng thu thập dữ liệu người dùng.

questions.question: Which method retrieves data from SharedPreferences?
questions.explanation: SharedPreferences dùng getString/getInt… (get).

questions.question: What is the main benefit of Flutter's widget-based architecture?

options.A: Widgets are only used for visual design
options.B: Widgets allow code reuse across platforms

questions.explanation: Widget tree + một codebase tái sử dụng qua nền tảng.

questions.question: Why are widgets lightweight in Flutter?
questions.explanation: Widget là mô tả bất biến, nhẹ; Element/RenderObject làm việc nặng.

questions.question: Why should authentication state be centralized?
questions.explanation: Auth tập trung → kiểm soát truy cập nhất quán toàn app.

questions.question: Which widget can hide user input text when configured for password entry?
questions.explanation: TextField với obscureText: true ẩn mật khẩu (ObscureText không phải widget).

questions.question: What is Flutter primarily used for?

options.B: Creating native applications from a single codebase
options.D: Building operating systems for mobile devices

questions.explanation: Flutter: app native-like đa nền tảng từ một codebase.

questions.question: Which method resets all form fields?
questions.explanation: FormState.reset() xóa/reset các field.

questions.question: Which Dart keyword is used to define a constant value at compile time?
questions.explanation: const = hằng compile-time; final = gán 1 lần runtime.

questions.question: What happens after successful login?
questions.explanation: Login thành công → bắt đầu phiên người dùng (session/token).

questions.question: Which step is required before running a Flutter app for the first time?
questions.explanation: Cần cài/setup Flutter SDK trước khi run app.

questions.question: Which widget reacts to async data from local storage?
questions.explanation: Local storage thường trả Future → FutureBuilder.

questions.question: What does golden testing verify?
questions.explanation: Golden test so sánh snapshot giao diện.

questions.question: Why should state be kept as local as possible?
questions.explanation: State càng local càng ít widget bị rebuild oan.

questions.question: What is the benefit of composing widgets in Flutter?

options.B: Better code reuse and UI consistency

questions.explanation: Ghép widget nhỏ → tái sử dụng và UI đồng nhất.

questions.question: Which status code indicates server error?
questions.explanation: 5xx = lỗi server; 500 Internal Server Error.

questions.question: According to Flutter architecture overview, which three layers are described?
questions.explanation: Slide M1: ba tầng Framework (Dart) – Engine (C++) – Embedder.

questions.question: What is the correct high-level rendering workflow in Flutter?

options.B: Widget Tree → Render Tree → Composited Scene → GPU Rendering
options.C: XML Layout → Bridge → Native View only

questions.explanation: M1: Widget Tree → Render Tree → Composited Scene → GPU.

questions.question: Which statement matches a key concept of Flutter UI?

options.A: Everything must be written in Kotlin
options.B: UI is defined only with HTML files
options.C: Everything in Flutter is a Widget

questions.explanation: M1: Everything in Flutter is a Widget.

questions.question: Which engine technology does Flutter use for rendering (as mentioned in slides)?
questions.explanation: M1 Engine: C++ powered by Skia, GPU acceleration.

questions.question: Compared with React Native in the comparison table, Flutter uses which language?
questions.explanation: M1 table: Flutter → Dart; React Native → JS.

questions.question: What is a main benefit of Flutter listed in the introduction slides?

options.A: Must rewrite UI for every OS with different languages only
options.B: Consistency: same UI across devices (plus performance/flexibility)

questions.explanation: M1 Benefits: Performance, Consistency, Flexibility.

questions.question: Which command verifies Flutter SDK installation after adding PATH?
questions.explanation: M1: Verify with flutter doctor.

questions.question: What is DartPad mainly useful for according to the slides?

options.A: Run Flutter/Dart code in browser without local install
options.B: Deploy apps to App Store automatically

questions.explanation: M1: DartPad online, no installation, ideal for demos.

questions.question: When installing Android Studio for Flutter, which plugin is required?

options.B: Flutter plugin (Dart plugin installs with it)

questions.explanation: M1: Install Flutter plugin; Dart comes automatically.

questions.question: Dart in Flutter is described as supporting which compilation modes?
questions.explanation: M1: Dart combines AOT and JIT.

questions.question: Which collection type stores ordered elements that may contain duplicates?
questions.explanation: M2: List ordered; Set unique; Map key-value.

questions.question: Which collection stores unique values only?
questions.explanation: M2: Set = unique elements.

questions.question: What does a Map primarily store in Dart?
questions.explanation: M2: Map: key-value.

questions.question: Which keyword creates a compile-time constant in Dart?
questions.explanation: const = compile-time constant; final = runtime single assignment.

questions.question: What is null safety designed to help with?

options.A: Prevent unexpected null errors by making types non-nullable by default
options.B: Force every variable to be null always

questions.explanation: M2/M1: null safety & strong typing.

questions.question: If a non-nullable variable is not initialized before use, what typically happens?
questions.explanation: Null safety: uninitialized non-nullable → compile error (FE style).

questions.question: Which statement about Dart classes is correct?

options.B: Dart is object-oriented; classes are blueprints for objects
options.D: Objects cannot be created from classes

questions.explanation: M2 OOP: classes/objects.

questions.question: What does string interpolation like "$name" do?

options.A: Embeds expression/variable values into a string

questions.explanation: Dart essentials: string interpolation.

questions.question: Which type annotation is appropriate for a list of integers?
questions.explanation: M2 example: List<int> nums = [1,2,3];

questions.question: Functions in Dart are described as:
questions.explanation: M1/M2: Functions as first-class objects.

questions.question: What is a common use of a factory constructor mentioned in slides?
questions.explanation: M3 Factory: singleton, fromJson, type checks.

questions.question: Why is async/await important in Flutter apps?

options.A: Handle asynchronous work without blocking the UI thread
options.B: Force UI to freeze during network calls

questions.explanation: Advanced Dart + Flutter: async avoids blocking UI.

questions.question: What does a Future represent?

options.C: A value or error that will be available later

questions.explanation: Future = async result later.

questions.question: What does a Stream provide compared to a single Future?

options.A: Exactly one value then ends always only
options.B: A sequence of asynchronous events/values over time

questions.explanation: Stream: multiple async events over time.

questions.question: How do packages and imports help structure Dart code?

options.B: Increase APK size without any reuse

questions.explanation: Packages/imports = modular reusable code.

questions.question: What is .fromJson() typically used for?

options.B: Converting JSON map data into a Dart model object

questions.explanation: M3 factory use cases: .fromJson().

questions.question: Which practice is recommended for long-running CPU work?

options.A: Run heavy loops directly in every build()
options.C: Use async patterns / avoid blocking UI (and isolates when needed)

questions.explanation: Advanced async + later M12: don't block UI.

questions.question: What does await do when used with a Future?

options.A: Suspends the async function until the Future completes

questions.explanation: await waits for Future completion inside async function.

questions.question: Which is a valid reason to use a singleton via factory?

options.A: To create millions of identical DB connections every frame
options.B: To reuse one shared service instance

questions.explanation: M3: factory singleton services.

questions.question: Generics in Dart mainly help you:
questions.explanation: Generics = reusable type-safe components (FE style).

questions.question: What is the role of BuildContext?

options.B: Locate a widget in the widget tree / access inherited widgets

questions.explanation: BuildContext: vị trí widget trong tree (FE + M4).

questions.question: What does setState() do in a StatefulWidget?

options.A: Marks state changed so Flutter rebuilds the widget

questions.explanation: setState → rebuild UI.

questions.question: Which widget arranges children vertically?
questions.explanation: Column = vertical; Row = horizontal.

questions.question: Which widget allows children to overlap?
questions.explanation: Stack overlaps children (M5 demo also uses Stack).

questions.question: What is Scaffold typically used for?
questions.explanation: Scaffold = screen structure.

questions.question: What does a Switch widget primarily toggle?
questions.explanation: M4: Switch toggles boolean (notifications, dark mode…).

questions.question: Why use const widgets when possible?

options.A: They force full tree rebuild every frame
options.C: They reduce rebuild cost for stable UI

questions.explanation: const widgets = cheaper rebuilds (FE + M12).

questions.question: What does ThemeData primarily define?
questions.explanation: ThemeData = visual theme (FE).

questions.question: What does the build() method return?

options.B: A widget tree describing the UI

questions.explanation: build() returns widgets describing UI.

questions.question: StatelessWidget is best when:

options.A: UI must hold mutable local state with setState
options.C: UI depends only on constructor inputs / configuration

questions.explanation: Stateless = no internal mutable state.

questions.question: crossAxisAlignment in a Column controls alignment along which axis?

options.A: Horizontal (cross axis) for a vertical Column

questions.explanation: Column main=vertical → cross=horizontal.

questions.question: Which widget is best for fixed empty space of a given size?
questions.explanation: SizedBox for fixed space (FE).

questions.question: What is a key idea of Navigator 2.0 in the slides?

options.A: Only imperative push/pop forever with no description of stack
options.B: Declarative model: describe the desired navigation stack

questions.explanation: M5: Navigator 2.0 declarative stack.

questions.question: What is deep linking used for?

options.A: Open a specific screen via URL/external source

questions.explanation: M5 Deep linking overview.

questions.question: Where do you typically configure Android deep link intent filters?
questions.explanation: M5: Modify AndroidManifest intent-filter.

questions.question: BLoC architecture primarily separates:

options.B: UI from business logic via events and states (often Streams)

questions.explanation: M5 state management: BLoC event→state.

questions.question: What is the output of a BLoC typically?
questions.explanation: BLoC emits States (FE).

questions.question: What do events do in BLoC?
questions.explanation: Events trigger processing → new states.

questions.question: Why is immutability important for BLoC states?
questions.explanation: Immutable states = predictable (FE).

questions.question: How can data be passed to a new screen in common Flutter practice?

options.C: Only by editing the Flutter engine

questions.explanation: Pass data via constructor/route args (FE).

questions.question: Which widget rebuilds when a Stream emits new data?
questions.explanation: StreamBuilder listens to Stream.

questions.question: Why separate UI and state logic?
questions.explanation: Separation → maintainability (FE B5).

questions.question: What does a route guard pattern aim to protect?
questions.explanation: Guard pattern for auth routes (FE B5).

questions.question: Provider is commonly used for:

options.B: Dependency injection / state exposure to the widget tree

questions.explanation: M5 ecosystem: Provider for DI/state.

questions.question: What is the main goal of responsive UI?

options.A: UI only works on one fixed phone model
options.B: Layouts adapt to different screen sizes

questions.explanation: M6: responsive & adaptive layouts.

questions.question: What does GridView help build according to the slides?

options.A: Scrollable grids that adapt columns to width

questions.explanation: M6 Responsive GridView.

questions.question: Which API is commonly used to read screen size/orientation?
questions.explanation: MediaQuery for size/orientation.

questions.question: Adaptive layout typically means:

options.A: Same exact pixels on all devices always
options.B: UI chooses suitable layout patterns for platform/screen class

questions.explanation: Adaptive = fit platform/screen class.

questions.question: Expanded inside a Flex (Row/Column) is used to:

options.A: Take remaining free space along the main axis

questions.explanation: Expanded fills remaining space.

questions.question: Why avoid hard-coded sizes for all devices?

options.A: Hard-coded sizes always look perfect everywhere
options.B: Screens differ; rigid sizes break layouts on small/large devices

questions.explanation: Responsive principle.

questions.question: LayoutBuilder is useful because it:

options.C: Provides parent constraints to choose layout

questions.explanation: LayoutBuilder → constraints-based UI.

questions.question: Which widget is commonly used for scrollable single-child content?
questions.explanation: SingleChildScrollView for overflow content.

questions.question: What is the primary purpose of forms in mobile apps?
questions.explanation: M7 + FE: forms collect user data.

questions.question: TextEditingController is used to:
questions.explanation: TextEditingController manages input value.

questions.question: How do you typically hide password characters in a TextField?
questions.explanation: obscureText on TextField (slides + FE).

questions.question: What does FormState.reset() do?

options.B: Reset form fields to initial values

questions.explanation: reset() clears/resets fields (FE).

questions.question: A strong password validation demo in slides requires at least:

options.A: Length rules (e.g. ≥ 8) and matching confirm password
options.D: Password stored in plain Text widget title only

questions.explanation: M7 Demo: password ≥ 8 & include constraints + confirm.

questions.question: GlobalKey<FormState> is commonly used to:
questions.explanation: Form key for validate/save/reset.

questions.question: Why validate user input before submit?

options.B: To ensure correct data and better UX / fewer server errors

questions.explanation: Validation improves UX and data quality.

questions.question: Async validation checks are useful when:

options.A: You must verify uniqueness against a server (e.g. username taken)

questions.explanation: M7 mentions async checks.

questions.question: Which widget groups multiple fields for joint validation?
questions.explanation: Form groups fields.

questions.question: AutovalidateMode.onUserInteraction typically means:

options.B: Validate as the user interacts with fields

questions.explanation: Auto-validate on interaction.

questions.question: Which HTTP method is typically used to retrieve data?
questions.explanation: GET retrieve (FE).

questions.question: What is the purpose of parsing JSON?
questions.explanation: Parse JSON → Dart models (FE + M8).

questions.question: Why is FutureBuilder useful for API data?
questions.explanation: M8: FutureBuilder binding data to UI.

questions.question: Which HTTP header commonly carries access credentials?
questions.explanation: Authorization header (FE).

questions.question: Which status code indicates a server error?
questions.explanation: 5xx server error (FE).

questions.question: What does status code 404 typically mean?
questions.explanation: 404 not found.

questions.question: What is pagination used for?

options.B: Loading large datasets in pages efficiently

questions.explanation: Pagination for large lists (FE).

questions.question: What is a refresh token used for?

options.C: Obtain new access tokens without re-login

questions.explanation: Refresh token extends session (FE + M10 overlap).

questions.question: When calling APIs, why handle loading and error states in UI?

options.A: Network is async; users need feedback and recovery paths
options.B: HTTP is always instant and never fails

questions.explanation: M8 FutureBuilder loading/success/error.

questions.question: POST is most appropriate when:

options.A: Idempotent pure retrieval of a static file only

questions.explanation: POST create/submit data.

questions.question: What does Content-Type: application/json indicate?

options.D: Request is a WebSocket upgrade only

questions.explanation: JSON content type.

questions.question: Why map JSON fields into model classes?

options.A: Type safety, readability, and easier UI binding
options.D: To force stringly-typed maps forever without structure

questions.explanation: Models + fromJson pattern.

questions.question: When are local files preferred over simple key–value storage?

options.B: When data is bigger/structured but may not need full SQL

questions.explanation: M9: files when bigger than key-value, structure without full SQL.

questions.question: Which package is commonly used for simple key-value storage?

options.D: path_provider only always as KV store

questions.explanation: shared_preferences for simple KV (FE).

questions.question: What is Hive mainly used for?
questions.explanation: Hive local NoSQL (FE).

questions.question: Which plugin provides SQLite support in Flutter?
questions.explanation: sqflite (FE).

questions.question: Which widget is often used to show async local storage reads?
questions.explanation: FutureBuilder + local async (FE).

questions.question: Why should database operations be asynchronous?

options.A: To freeze the UI on purpose
options.B: To avoid blocking the UI thread
options.C: Because SQL cannot run async ever

questions.explanation: Async DB → keep UI responsive (FE).

questions.question: path_provider is typically used to:

options.C: Locate app directories for file storage

questions.explanation: path_provider for filesystem locations.

questions.question: Which storage suits a small flag like 'onboardingCompleted'?
questions.explanation: Small KV → SharedPreferences.

questions.question: What is persistence in mobile apps?

options.A: Keeping data only in RAM until process death always
options.B: Saving data so it survives app restarts

questions.explanation: Persistence = survive restarts.

questions.question: Choosing SQLite is more appropriate when:

options.C: You need relational queries on structured local data

questions.explanation: SQLite for relational local data.

questions.question: Why is secure authentication critical in mobile apps?

options.B: To protect user data and control access

questions.explanation: Auth protects user data (FE).

questions.question: After successful login with a mock token flow, what should you typically do?

options.A: Save token (e.g. in AuthService) and prepare next navigation

questions.explanation: M10 steps: receive token → save → continue.

questions.question: What is a token in authentication systems?
questions.explanation: Token = access credential (FE).

questions.question: Why centralize authentication state?

options.A: To scatter tokens in random widgets
options.B: Ensure consistent access control across screens

questions.explanation: Central auth state (FE).

questions.question: Which widget can react to auth state exposed as a Stream?
questions.explanation: Auth stream → StreamBuilder (FE).

questions.question: What happens after successful login from a session perspective?

options.D: All tokens are printed to UI permanently

questions.explanation: Session starts (FE).

questions.question: Storing tokens insecurely (plain SharedPreferences without care) risks:
questions.explanation: Security: protect tokens/session.

questions.question: Logout should typically:

options.A: Clear session/token and return to unauthenticated UI

questions.explanation: Logout clears session.

questions.question: Push notifications in this module are related to:

options.B: Alerting users (often after auth/session context exists)

questions.explanation: M10 includes notifications with auth/session themes.

questions.question: Protected routes should:
questions.explanation: Route protection / guard idea.

questions.question: Which test type checks individual functions or classes?
questions.explanation: Unit test (FE + M11).

questions.question: Which Flutter test focuses on UI components/widgets?

options.A: Unit test only of pure Dart math

questions.explanation: Widget test (FE).

questions.question: What does an integration test verify?
questions.explanation: Integration ≈ E2E behavior (FE).

questions.question: Why are tests important for refactoring?

options.A: They slow CI only with no benefit
options.D: They remove the need for UI

questions.explanation: Tests protect refactoring (FE B5).

questions.question: What is test isolation?

options.B: Running all tests in one shared mutable global only

questions.explanation: Isolation (FE).

questions.question: Which tool helps debug Flutter apps (network, performance, etc.)?
questions.explanation: DevTools (FE + M11).

questions.question: The Taskly demo app in slides is used to:

options.B: Demonstrate each test type on a simple Todo app

questions.explanation: M11 Taskly demo for tests.

questions.question: find.byType / find.text in widget tests are used to:

options.A: Locate widgets in the test environment

questions.explanation: Widget test finders.

questions.question: Mocking dependencies in unit tests helps:

options.C: Test logic in isolation without external systems

questions.explanation: Mocks isolate unit logic.

questions.question: Golden tests primarily verify:
questions.explanation: Golden = UI appearance (FE; extended knowledge).

questions.question: What should you avoid doing inside build()?

options.A: Heavy work: sorting, JSON parse, complex calc, DB/API calls

questions.explanation: M12: Avoid heavy work in build().

questions.question: Why extract list item widgets (e.g. TaskTile) and use Selector?

options.A: To rebuild the entire screen on every tiny change always
options.B: To reduce unnecessary rebuilds / narrow rebuild scope

questions.explanation: M12 Taskly: widget extraction + Selector.

questions.question: What is the main purpose of hot reload?

options.C: Instantly reflect code changes in UI while developing

questions.explanation: Hot reload (FE + intro/M12).

questions.question: Which command builds a release APK?
questions.explanation: flutter build apk (FE).

questions.question: Why use ValueKey(task.id) instead of UniqueKey() for list items?

options.A: Preserve identity/state; UniqueKey forces excess rebuilds

questions.explanation: M12 Keys: ValueKey good; UniqueKey bad for lists.

questions.question: GlobalKey warning in slides: GlobalKey is:

options.A: Always free and should be used everywhere
options.B: Powerful but expensive; avoid unless necessary

questions.explanation: M12: GlobalKey powerful but slow.

questions.question: Which practice improves deployment security?

options.C: Protecting signing keys / secure release process
options.D: Using only debug builds in production

questions.explanation: Secure signing keys (FE).

questions.question: const widgets help performance because they:

options.A: Can be reused and reduce rebuild work

questions.explanation: const + extraction (M12).

questions.question: What is technical debt?

options.B: Future cost of poor design decisions

questions.explanation: Technical debt (FE wording; design quality).

questions.question: Why is clean architecture beneficial?

options.A: Easier maintenance and testing via separation of concerns
options.B: Fewer files always regardless of design

questions.explanation: Clean architecture benefits (FE).

questions.question: App size analysis before deployment helps you:

options.C: Find large assets/code to optimize release package

questions.explanation: M12 focus: app size analysis / deploy prep.

questions.question: Why decide architecture early?
questions.explanation: Early architecture reduces refactor cost (FE).
